title: Chapter 12 Quiz: Publishing
description: Test your knowledge of production preparation, building with EAS, code signing, and store submission.

questions.question: Where do you configure the app icon in an Expo project?
questions.explanation: The icon is set in the `expo` section of app.json.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the splash screen?
options: To display while the app loads
To navigate to the home screen
questions.explanation: The splash screen is shown during the initial loading phase.

questions.question: Which command initialises EAS Build configuration?
questions.explanation: `eas build:configure` creates eas.json.

questions.question: What is the iOS signing requirement for distributing apps?
options: Apple Developer Program membership and provisioning profile
A code signing certificate from any CA
questions.explanation: iOS requires an Apple Developer account and proper provisioning.

questions.question: Why is it critical to back up your Android keystore?
options: It contains the app's source code
Without it, you cannot publish updates to the same app
questions.explanation: The keystore is used to sign your app; losing it prevents future updates.

questions.question: What does EAS Submit do?
options: Uploads the app to app stores
questions.explanation: EAS Submit handles store uploads.

questions.question: Which EAS service allows pushing JavaScript updates without a new store submission?
questions.explanation: EAS Update provides over‑the‑air updates.

questions.question: In `app.json`, what field sets the unique identifier for an Android app?
questions.explanation: `android.package` is the unique application ID.

questions.question: What is the recommended minimum icon size for iOS?
questions.explanation: iOS requires a 1024x1024 icon for the App Store.

questions.question: What does `eas.json` contain?
questions.explanation: eas.json defines build profiles (development, preview, production).

questions.question: You need an Apple Developer Program membership to build an iOS app for the App Store.
questions.explanation: Yes, it costs $99/year and is required for distribution.

questions.question: EAS Build can only build apps for iOS.
questions.explanation: EAS Build supports both iOS and Android.

questions.question: OTA updates can change native code like adding a new library.
questions.explanation: OTA updates only affect JavaScript and assets; native changes require a full build.

questions.question: You can submit your app to Google Play without a keystore.
questions.explanation: All Android apps must be signed with a keystore.

questions.question: The command to create a production build with EAS for iOS is `eas build -p ios --profile _____`.
questions.explanation: The profile name is typically 'production'.

questions.question: The unique identifier for an iOS app is called the _____.
questions.explanation: It's set in `ios.bundleIdentifier` in app.json.

questions.question: What is the purpose of `runtimeVersion` in app.json for EAS Update?
options: To specify the minimum iOS version
To indicate which native version the JS bundle is compatible with
To set the app's version number
questions.explanation: `runtimeVersion` ensures that updates are only applied to compatible native binaries.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT required for a Google Play Store listing?
questions.explanation: A preview video is optional; the others are required.

questions.question: Where are store listings (description, screenshots) managed?
options: On the app store consoles (App Store Connect / Google Play Console)
questions.explanation: Store metadata is entered directly on the store platforms.

questions.question: What does EAS Update require in the project to function?
questions.explanation: You need to install and configure `expo-updates`.
